content: We can repurpose the lossless static representation property of temporal event graphs to design a lossless context compression framework for large language model finite context windows, where ordered interaction events are structured as edges and core entities as nodes instead of being stored as raw sequential tokens. This structured representation automatically compresses redundant raw token overhead while retaining all full temporal dependency information of the extended context, sidestepping the information loss tradeoff that plagues current sliding-window and summarization-based context compression methods.
